scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: fetched Veeqo's complete published OpenAPI 3.0 document (developers.veeqo.com/api-schemas/veeqo-api/, embedded inline JSON) plus every individual operation doc page from the site's sitemap (developers.veeqo.com/sitemap-0.xml) to recover detail/update/delete routes not present in the base schema (e.g. GET/PUT /orders/{order_id}, GET/PUT/DELETE /products/{product_id}, GET/PUT /warehouses/{id}). Covered: 9 read streams (orders plus 8 page/page_size-paginated collections: products, customers, warehouses, suppliers, purchase_orders, channels, delivery_methods, tags) and 24 write actions. Corrected from an earlier pass of this file: orders/products/customers/payments/shipments create-or-update bodies are documented NESTED (or partially-nested) envelopes (e.g. {"customer": {...}}, {"order": {...line_items_attributes...}}, {"amount": ..., "payment_attributes": {...}}) — this IS fully expressible in this dialect (body_type: json serializes every top-level record_schema key verbatim, and one of those keys can itself be an object; see yotpo/teamtailor/teamwork's identical envelope-shaped writes elsewhere in this repo), so create_customer/update_customer/create_product/update_product/create_order/update_order/cancel_order/create_payment/create_shipment are all real write actions, not an ENGINE_GAP exclusion (a prior pass of this file incorrectly excluded them on that claim). Remaining exclusions, every one with a real, specific per-endpoint category: single-item detail GETs duplicating an already-covered list stream (duplicate_of); per-order/per-product/per-bundle/per-sellable sub-resources with no top-level list endpoint of their own, which would require per-parent fan_out this pass does not scope (out_of_scope); carrier label-purchase/rate-shopping actions requiring configured carrier credentials beyond plain API-key auth (requires_elevated_scope); binary PDF/PNG label downloads (binary_payload); and account-profile endpoints (non_data_endpoint).

per-bundle contents sub-resource create; breadth-vs-cost triage

per-bundle-content removal; narrow sub-resource mutation, breadth-vs-cost triage

single-channel (store) detail; identical record shape to the channels list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
